content_markdown: A source emits sound of frequency $500\text{ Hz}$. The source approaches a stationary observer at $30\text{ m s}^{-1}$. The speed of sound is $330\text{ m s}^{-1}$. What is the frequency of the sound detected by the observer?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

vibe_explanation: Since the source is moving towards a stationary observer, we use the Doppler effect formula for a moving source: $f' = f \left( \frac{v}{v - v_s} \right)$. Plugging in the values: $f' = 500 \left( \frac{330}{330 - 30} \right) = 500 \left( \frac{330}{300} \right) = 500 \times 1.1 = 550\text{ Hz}$.
